topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge known as The Boat Race.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
Cambridge won the race by 35 lengths, as of 2014 the largest winning margin in the history of the event.
Document 2: The Boat Race is an annual rowing eight competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
Cambridge won the race by 20 lengths to level the overall record at 1 – 1.
Document 3: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames in London.
Cambridge won by a quarter of a length, the narrowest margin of victory since the 1877 race.

topic.name: fungal and plant taxonomy
topic.description: The documents discuss the classification and characteristics of various fungi and flowering plants, emphasizing their taxonomy and ecological roles.

Document 1: Mycena stylobates, commonly known as the bulbous bonnet, is a species of inedible mushroom in the family Mycenaceae.
The species was first named Agaricus stylobates by Christian Hendrik Persoon in 1801.
Document 2: The lily family, Liliaceae, consists of fifteen genera and about 705 known species of flowering plants within the order Liliales.
First described in 1789, the lily family became a paraphyletic 'catch-all' group of petaloid monocots.
Document 3: Suillus americanus is a species of fungus in the Suillaceae family of mushrooms.
Suillus americanus was first described scientifically by American mycologist Charles Horton Peck in 1888.

topic.name: Volcanic Activity and Water Bodies
topic.description: This topic encompasses the formation and characteristics of caldera lakes and dams resulting from volcanic activity and hydrological engineering.

Document 1: Crater Lake is a caldera lake in the western United States, located in south-central Oregon.
The lake partly fills a nearly 2,148-foot deep caldera that was formed around 7,700 years ago by the collapse of the volcano Mount Mazama.
Document 2: The Pensacola Dam is a multiple-arch buttress dam on the Grand River in-between Disney and Langley in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.
The dam was constructed for the purposes of hydroelectric power generation, flood control and recreation.
Document 3: Incapillo is a Pleistocene caldera, a depression formed by the collapse of a volcano, in the La Rioja province of Argentina.
Incapillo is considered the southernmost volcanic centre in the Central Volcanic Zone of the Andes with Pleistocene activity.
